content:
February 29 is known as a leap day (or “leap year day”), which is periodically added as the last day of the month to the Julian and Gregorian calendars, as an intercalary date, to create leap years. Its main function is to keep the calendars aligned with the Earth’s seasons, as the planet orbits the sun.
~Wikipedia
Oh wait, you wanted know about me ? # Hi, I am Prakhar Sengupta, # a Software Engineer usually working on Large Scale Integration Systems, Asset Management Solutions, General Programming and other relevant things.
Currently working at IBM-India.
This website serves as a place to showcase my work and other stuffs, also a possible graveyard of all failed things and so on.

About # Sometimes I go about in pity for myself, and all the while, a great wind carries me across the sky
~Ojibwe Saying
Interested in all things related to tech, literature, film and music. Lately I have been thinking of investing into Astatine.
I have also dabbled a bit in music production, check out my stuff over here.
If you are interested in my works, professional or otherwise, check out the Work page, for some random collection of things, check out Stuff.

Changelog # All notable changes to this website will be documented over here.
The format is based on Keep a Changelog
For quick look at current version info, head to Version.
[0.1.2] - 2026-04-14 # General # Several changes across the board, namely addition of all the pages, rework of the homepage, animation refining. Homepage # Default card layout with animation [0.1.1] - 2026-04-13 # General # Created website prototype. Much more features to come.

this place seems a tad bit empty … ?
TODO # Remove shadows and rounded edges from homepage card

You can find records of my work over here, both professional and personal. I might have missed out on archiving my lots of other engagements, maybe you can find em floating somwhere around the interwebz.
Professional Work # Software Engineer | IBM | KOL, WB # April 2025 - Present
Maintaining and providing enhancement to a Client’s flagship application suite, integrated using IBM MQ. Worked on providing major optimizations to client’s existing Maximo system.
Developed major features for client’s Asset Management System and undertook crucial design decision to optimize existing workflows. Optimized database queries and indexing strategies, reducing response times by almost 50% Collaborated with cross-functional teams to deliver features on time and within scope Provide upto L3 level of support across multiple different levels of services Worked in multiple engagements and projects, namely Major overhaul and upgrade of the Integration service Associate Software Engineer | IBM | BLR, KA # January 2024 - March 2025
Joined as a graduate hire and worked on IBM’s proprietary EAM software, Maximo 7.6.x. Was engaged in delivering Asset Management Solutions to ANZ’s one of the leading Integrated Transport Service.
Provided L1 / L2 level of support towards third party services on standalone servers Developed minor feature releases for client’s existing Asset Management System Fixed bugs and implemented custom enhancement requests based on user requests and client deliverables Personal Work # As of late I am trying to develop a no BS route planning solution. Would like any help anyone can offer around this.
